result.stdout:
Description:
  nbgv v3.10.8-alpha+dbddc0ac52

Nutzung:
  nbgv [command] [options]

Optionen:
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information
  --version       Versionsinformationen anzeigen

Befehle:
  install                   Prepares a project to have version stamps applied using Nerdbank.GitVersioning.
  get-version <commit-ish>  Gets the version information for a project. [default: HEAD]
  set-version <version>     Updates the version stamp that is applied to a project.
  tag <versionOrRef>        Creates a git tag to mark a version. [default: HEAD]
  get-commits <version>     Gets the commit(s) that match a given version.
  cloud                     Communicates with the ambient cloud build to set the build number and/or other cloud build variables.
  prepare-release <tag>     Prepares a release by creating a release branch for the current version and adjusting the version on the current branch.

  nbgv [command] [options]

Optionen:
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information
  --version       Versionsinformationen anzeigen

Befehle:
  install                   Prepares a project to have version stamps applied using Nerdbank.GitVersioning.
  get-version <commit-ish>  Gets the version information for a project. [default: HEAD]
  set-version <version>     Updates the version stamp that is applied to a project.
  tag <versionOrRef>        Creates a git tag to mark a version. [default: HEAD]
  get-commits <version>     Gets the commit(s) that match a given version.
  cloud                     Communicates with the ambient cloud build to set the build number and/or other cloud build variables.
  prepare-release <tag>     Prepares a release by creating a release branch for the current version and adjusting the version on the current branch.

result.stdout:
Description:
  Communicates with the ambient cloud build to set the build number and/or other cloud build variables.

Nutzung:
  nbgv cloud [options]

Optionen:
  -p, --project <project>      The path to the project or project directory used to calculate the version. The default is the current directory. Ignored if the -v option is specified.
  --metadata <metadata>        Adds an identifier to the build metadata part of a semantic version.
  -v, --version <version>      The string to use for the cloud build number. If not specified, the computed version will be used.
  -s, --ci-system <ci-system>  Force activation for a particular CI system. If not specified, auto-detection will be used. Supported values are: AppVeyor, VisualStudioTeamServices, GitHubActions, TeamCity, AtlassianBamboo, Jenkins, GitLab, Travis, SpaceAutomation, BitbucketCloud
  -a, --all-vars               Defines ALL version variables as cloud build variables, with a "NBGV_" prefix.
  -c, --common-vars            Defines a few common version variables as cloud build variables, with a "Git" prefix (e.g. GitBuildVersion, GitBuildVersionSimple, GitAssemblyInformationalVersion).
  --skip-cloud-build-number    Do not emit the cloud build variable to set the build number. This is useful when you want to set other cloud build variables but not the build number.
  -d, --define <define>        Additional cloud build variables to define. Each should be in the NAME=VALUE syntax.
  -?, -h, --help               Show help and usage information

commands.payload:
Description:
  Communicates with the ambient cloud build to set the build number and/or other cloud build variables.

Nutzung:
  nbgv cloud [options]

Optionen:
  -p, --project <project>      The path to the project or project directory used to calculate the version. The default is the current directory. Ignored if the -v option is specified.
  --metadata <metadata>        Adds an identifier to the build metadata part of a semantic version.
  -v, --version <version>      The string to use for the cloud build number. If not specified, the computed version will be used.
  -s, --ci-system <ci-system>  Force activation for a particular CI system. If not specified, auto-detection will be used. Supported values are: AppVeyor, VisualStudioTeamServices, GitHubActions, TeamCity, AtlassianBamboo, Jenkins, GitLab, Travis, SpaceAutomation, BitbucketCloud
  -a, --all-vars               Defines ALL version variables as cloud build variables, with a "NBGV_" prefix.
  -c, --common-vars            Defines a few common version variables as cloud build variables, with a "Git" prefix (e.g. GitBuildVersion, GitBuildVersionSimple, GitAssemblyInformationalVersion).
  --skip-cloud-build-number    Do not emit the cloud build variable to set the build number. This is useful when you want to set other cloud build variables but not the build number.
  -d, --define <define>        Additional cloud build variables to define. Each should be in the NAME=VALUE syntax.
  -?, -h, --help               Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Gets the commit(s) that match a given version.

Nutzung:
  nbgv get-commits <version> [options]

Argumente:
  <version>  The a.b.c[.d] version to find.

Optionen:
  -p, --project <project>  The path to the project or project directory. The default is the root directory of the repo that spans the current directory, or an existing version.json file, if applicable.
  -q, --quiet              Use minimal output.
  -?, -h, --help           Show help and usage information

  -q, --quiet              Use minimal output.
  -?, -h, --help           Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Gets the version information for a project.

Nutzung:
  nbgv get-version [<commit-ish>] [options]

Argumente:
  <commit-ish>  The commit/ref to get the version information for. [default: HEAD]

Optionen:
  -p, --project <project>    The path to the project or project directory. The default is the current directory.
  --metadata <metadata>      Adds an identifier to the build metadata part of a semantic version.
  -f, --format <format>      The format to write the version information. Allowed values are: text, json. The default is text.
  -v, --variable <variable>  The name of just one version property to print to stdout. When specified, the output is always in raw text. Useful in scripts.
  --public-release           Specifies whether this is a public release. When specified, overrides the PublicRelease environment variable. Use --public-release=true or --public-release=false to explicitly set the value.
  -?, -h, --help             Show help and usage information

commands.payload:
Description:
  Gets the version information for a project.

Nutzung:
  nbgv get-version [<commit-ish>] [options]

Argumente:
  <commit-ish>  The commit/ref to get the version information for. [default: HEAD]

Optionen:
  -p, --project <project>    The path to the project or project directory. The default is the current directory.
  --metadata <metadata>      Adds an identifier to the build metadata part of a semantic version.
  -f, --format <format>      The format to write the version information. Allowed values are: text, json. The default is text.
  -v, --variable <variable>  The name of just one version property to print to stdout. When specified, the output is always in raw text. Useful in scripts.
  --public-release           Specifies whether this is a public release. When specified, overrides the PublicRelease environment variable. Use --public-release=true or --public-release=false to explicitly set the value.
  -?, -h, --help             Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Prepares a project to have version stamps applied using Nerdbank.GitVersioning.

Nutzung:
  nbgv install [options]

Optionen:
  -p, --path <path>        The path to the directory that should contain the version.json file. The default is the root of the git repo.
  -v, --version <version>  The initial version to set. [default: 1.0-beta]
  -s, --source <source>    The URI(s) of the NuGet package source(s) used to determine the latest stable version of the Nerdbank.GitVersioning package. This setting overrides all of the sources specified in the NuGet.Config files.
  -?, -h, --help           Show help and usage information

commands.payload:
Description:
  Prepares a project to have version stamps applied using Nerdbank.GitVersioning.

Nutzung:

Optionen:

  -v, --version <version>  The initial version to set. [default: 1.0-beta]
  -s, --source <source>    The URI(s) of the NuGet package source(s) used to determine the latest stable version of the Nerdbank.GitVersioning package. This setting overrides all of the sources specified in the NuGet.Config files.
  -?, -h, --help           Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Prepares a release by creating a release branch for the current version and adjusting the version on the current branch.

Nutzung:
  nbgv prepare-release [<tag>] [options]

Argumente:
  <tag>  The prerelease tag to apply on the release branch (if any). If not specified, any existing prerelease tag will be removed. The preceding hyphen may be omitted.

Optionen:
  -p, --project <project>                            The path to the project or project directory. The default is the current directory.
  --nextVersion <nextVersion>                        The version to set for the current branch. If omitted, the next version is determined automatically by incrementing the current version.
  --versionIncrement <versionIncrement>              Overrides the 'versionIncrement' setting set in version.json for determining the next version of the current branch.
  -f, --format <format>                              The format to write information about the release. Allowed values are: text, json. The default is text.
  --commit-message-pattern <commit-message-pattern>  A custom message to use for the commit that changes the version number. May include {0} for the version number. If not specified, the default is "Set version to '{0}'".
  -?, -h, --help                                     Show help and usage information

commands.payload:
Description:
  Prepares a release by creating a release branch for the current version and adjusting the version on the current branch.

Nutzung:
  nbgv prepare-release [<tag>] [options]

Argumente:
  <tag>  The prerelease tag to apply on the release branch (if any). If not specified, any existing prerelease tag will be removed. The preceding hyphen may be omitted.

Optionen:
  -p, --project <project>                            The path to the project or project directory. The default is the current directory.
  --nextVersion <nextVersion>                        The version to set for the current branch. If omitted, the next version is determined automatically by incrementing the current version.
  --versionIncrement <versionIncrement>              Overrides the 'versionIncrement' setting set in version.json for determining the next version of the current branch.
  -f, --format <format>                              The format to write information about the release. Allowed values are: text, json. The default is text.
  --commit-message-pattern <commit-message-pattern>  A custom message to use for the commit that changes the version number. May include {0} for the version number. If not specified, the default is "Set version to '{0}'".
  -?, -h, --help                                     Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Creates a git tag to mark a version.

Nutzung:
  nbgv tag [<versionOrRef>] [options]

Argumente:
  <versionOrRef>  The a.b.c[.d] version or git ref to be tagged. [default: HEAD]

Optionen:
  -p, --project <project>  The path to the project or project directory. The default is the root directory of the repo that spans the current directory, or an existing version.json file, if applicable.
  --what-if                Calculates and outputs the tag name without creating the tag.
  -?, -h, --help           Show help and usage information

commands.payload:
Description:
  Creates a git tag to mark a version.

Nutzung:
  nbgv tag [<versionOrRef>] [options]

Argumente:
  <versionOrRef>  The a.b.c[.d] version or git ref to be tagged. [default: HEAD]

Optionen:
  -p, --project <project>  The path to the project or project directory. The default is the root directory of the repo that spans the current directory, or an existing version.json file, if applicable.
  --what-if                Calculates and outputs the tag name without creating the tag.
  -?, -h, --help           Show help and usage information
